---
title: "Project 3"
author: "Lu Bai"
date: "2026-04-18"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
# Load libraries
library(survival)
library(tidyverse)
library(tableone)
library(flextable)
library(survminer) # For KM curve
library(MASS) # For stepAIC (Backward Selection)
library(broom)
library(ggplot2)
```

# Data cleaning and management
```{r}
# Load data
framingham <- read.csv("~/Downloads/frmgham2.csv")

# Data cleaning and management
framingham1 <- framingham %>% filter(PERIOD == 1,
                                     PREVSTRK == 0)

fram_10yr <- framingham1 %>%
  mutate(
    # Time to event is the minimum of stroke date, death date, or 10 years
    # Note: If stroke occurs before death, it is an event.
    fotime = pmin(TIMESTRK, TIMEDTH, 3652, na.rm = TRUE),
    strk_event = if_else(TIMESTRK <= 3652 & STROKE == 1, 1, 0),
    dth_event = if_else(TIMEDTH <= 3652 & STROKE == 0 & DEATH == 1, 1, 0)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(SEX, AGE, DIABETES, SYSBP)
#, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, TOTCHOL, BMI
```

# Generate table 1
```{r}
# Define variables for the table
vars <- c("strk_event", "dth_event", "AGE", "SYSBP", "TOTCHOL", "BMI", "DIABETES", "PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE")
factorVars <- c("strk_event", "dth_event", "DIABETES", "PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE")

# Create the summary stratified by Sex
tab1_obj <- CreateTableOne(vars = vars, 
                           strata = "SEX", 
                           data = fram_10yr, 
                           factorVars = factorVars,
                           test = TRUE,
                           addOverall = TRUE)

# Generate table 1 using flextable
tab1_df <- print(tab1_obj, 
                 missing = TRUE,
                 #quote = FALSE, 
                 noSpaces = TRUE, 
                 printToggle = FALSE,
                 showAllLevels = FALSE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Characteristic") %>%
  dplyr::select(-c(test))

# Rename rows and columns of table 1
tab1_df$Characteristic <- c("N", "Stroke event, n(%)", "Death, n(%)",
                            "Age, mean(SD)", "Systolic blood pressure, mean(SD)",
                            "Total cholesterol, mean(SD)", "BMI, mean(SD)", 
                            "Diabetic, n(%)","Prevalent coronary heart disease, n(%)",
                            "Use of anti-hypertensive meds, n(%)", "Smoking, n(%)")
colnames(tab1_df) <- c("Characteristic", "Overall", 
                      "Men", "Women", "p-value",
                      "Rate of Missing (%)")

ft1 <- flextable(tab1_df) %>%
  set_header_labels(Characteristic = "Baseline Characteristic") %>%
  bold(part = "header") %>%
  align(j = 1:6, align = "center", part = "body") %>%
  #italic(i = ~ str_detect(Characteristic, "mean|%"), j = 1) %>%
  autofit() %>%
  add_footer_lines("Note: Stroke event indicates participants had the first occurrence of stroke within the 10-year follow-up; Censoring in stroke event includes death without stroke and completion of 10-year follow-up; Death indicates participants die within the 10-year follow-up.") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  set_caption("Table 1. Descriptive statistics of baseline characteristics, the number of stroke and death event by sex")

# Display table
ft1
```

# Kaplan-Meier Curves
```{r}
# Fit KM Curve for Secondary risk factors
km_chd <- survfit(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ PREVCHD, data = fram_10yr)
km_bpmeds <- survfit(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ BPMEDS, data = fram_10yr)
km_smoke <- survfit(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ CURSMOKE, data = fram_10yr)

# Generate individual plots
plot_chd <- ggsurvplot(km_chd, data = fram_10yr, break.time.by = 365,
                       xlim = c(0, 3652.5), ylim = c(0.8, 1), 
                       title = "Prevalent CHD", font.title = c(12),
                       legend.labs = c("No", "Yes"), 
                       xlab = "Days of Follow-up", 
                       ylab = "Stroke-Free Survival Probability",
                       ggtheme = theme_minimal())

plot_bpmeds <- ggsurvplot(km_bpmeds, data = fram_10yr, break.time.by = 365,
                          xlim = c(0, 3652.5), ylim = c(0.8, 1),
                          title = "Use of Anti-hypertensive Meds", font.title = c(12),
                          xlab = "Days of Follow-up",
                          ylab = "Stroke-Free Survival Probability",
                          legend.labs = c("No", "Yes"), ggtheme = theme_minimal())

plot_smoke <- ggsurvplot(km_smoke, data = fram_10yr, break.time.by = 365,
                         xlim = c(0, 3652.5), ylim = c(0.8, 1),
                         title = "Current Smoking", font.title = c(12),
                         xlab = "Days of Follow-up",
                         ylab = "Stroke-Free Survival Probability",
                         legend.labs = c("Non-Smoker", "Smoker"), ggtheme = theme_minimal())

# Arrange in two columns
splots <- list(plot_chd, plot_bpmeds, plot_smoke)
arrange_ggsurvplots(splots, print = TRUE, ncol = 2, nrow = 2,
                    title = "Figure 1. 10-Year Stroke-Free Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Secondary Risk Factors",
                    font.main = c(18, "bold"))
```

# Secondary risk factor backward selection
```{r}
# Model selection (Backward)
# Fit full model with all primary and secondary factors for Males
# Define all variables involved in the selection process
vars_to_keep <- c("AGE", "DIABETES", "SYSBP", "PREVCHD", 
                  "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "TOTCHOL", "BMI", 
                  "fotime", "strk_event")

# Create a clean subset for the female-stratified analysis
dat_complete <- fram_10yr %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(vars_to_keep)) %>%
  drop_na() 

mod_full <- coxph(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP + PREVCHD + BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI, data = dat_complete)

# Perform backward selection based on AIC
mod_final <- step(mod_full, direction = "backward")
```

```{r}
# Manually create the data frame based on stepwise output
aic_summary <- data.frame(
  Step = c("Start", "Step 1", "Step 2", "Step 3", "Step 4 (Final)"),
  Model_Formula = c(
    "Full Model (All Variables)",
    "- TOTCHOL",
    "- BMI",
    "- PREVCHD",
    "- BPMEDS"
  ),
  AIC = c(1611.4, 1609.56, 1607.79, 1606.62, 1606.52),
  Variables_Remaining = c(
    "All", 
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, BMI, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, Smoke",
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, Smoke",
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, BPMEDS, Smoke",
    "Age, Diabetes, SYSBP, Smoke"
  )
)

# Generate a summary table
aic_table <- flextable(aic_summary) %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Step = "Iteration",
    Model_Formula = "Variable Removed",
    AIC = "AIC Value",
    Variables_Remaining = "Predictors"
  ) %>%
  bold(i = 5, part = "body") %>%
  bg(i = 5, bg = "#f2f2f2", part = "body") %>%
  set_caption("Table 2. Summary of Backward Model Selection via AIC") %>%
  autofit() %>%
  theme_booktabs()

# Display the table
aic_table
```

# Fit final model

```{r}
# Fit final model
mod_male <- coxph(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP + CURSMOKE, data = filter(fram_10yr, SEX == 1))

mod_female <- coxph(Surv(fotime, strk_event) ~ AGE + DIABETES + SYSBP + CURSMOKE, data = filter(fram_10yr, SEX == 2))

# Extract and clean the results for both models
# calculate Hazard Ratios (exp(coef)) and 95% Confidence Intervals
tidy_male <- tidy(mod_male, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(Model = "Male Cox PH Model")

tidy_female <- tidy(mod_female, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(Model = "Female Cox PH Model")

# Combine the data
summary_df <- bind_rows(tidy_male, tidy_female) %>%
  dplyr::select(Model, term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~round(., 3)),
    p.value = if_else(p.value < 0.001, "< 0.001", as.character(round(p.value, 3))),
    HR_CI = paste0(estimate, " (", conf.low, "-", conf.high, ")")
  )

# Create the Flextable
tab_mod_summary <- summary_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Model, term, HR_CI, p.value) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  merge_v(j = "Model") %>%
  set_header_labels(
    term = "Risk Factor",
    HR_CI = "Hazard Ratio (95% CI)",
    p.value = "P-Value"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Table 2: Adjusted Hazard Ratios for 10-Year Stroke Risk by Sex") %>%
  bold(i = ~ as.numeric(gsub("< ", "", p.value)) < 0.05, j = "p.value") %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  valign(j = "Model", valign = "top") %>%
  add_footer_lines("Note: Models adjusted for Age, Diabetes, Systolic BP, and Current Smoking status based on backward AIC selection.") %>%
  autofit()

tab_mod_summary
```

# Calculate 10-year probability of stroke
```{r}
# Calculate 10-Year stroke probability
# Function to extract 10-year probability of stroke from models
get_prob <- function(model, age, sbp, diabetes, smoke) {
  profile <- data.frame(AGE = age, SYSBP = sbp, DIABETES = diabetes, CURSMOKE = smoke)
  surv_est <- survfit(model, newdata = profile)
  prob <- 1 - summary(surv_est, times = 3652)$surv
  return(round(prob * 100, 2))
}

# Construct the results data frame
profile_df <- expand.grid(
  Age = c(40, 50, 60),
  # Baseline is SBP=110, Diabetes=0, Smoke=0. High BP is SBP=170.
  Scenario = c("Baseline", "High BP (>160 mmHg)", "Diabetes", "High BP + Diabetes", "Smoking")
) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    Male_Prob = case_when(
      Scenario == "Baseline" ~ get_prob(mod_male, Age, 110, 0, 0),
      Scenario == "High BP (>160 mmHg)" ~ get_prob(mod_male, Age, 170, 0, 0),
      Scenario == "Diabetes" ~ get_prob(mod_male, Age, 110, 1, 0),
      Scenario == "High BP + Diabetes" ~ get_prob(mod_male, Age, 170, 1, 0),
      Scenario == "Smoking" ~ get_prob(mod_male, Age, 110, 0, 1)
    ),
    Female_Prob = case_when(
      Scenario == "Baseline" ~ get_prob(mod_female, Age, 110, 0, 0),
      Scenario == "High BP (>160 mmHg)" ~ get_prob(mod_female, Age, 170, 0, 0),
      Scenario == "Diabetes" ~ get_prob(mod_female, Age, 110, 1, 0),
      Scenario == "High BP + Diabetes" ~ get_prob(mod_female, Age, 170, 1, 0),
      Scenario == "Smoking" ~ get_prob(mod_female, Age, 110, 0, 1)
    )
  )

# Pivot the data to have age as columns
df_male_wide <- profile_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Age, Scenario, Male_Prob) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Age, values_from = Male_Prob, names_prefix = "Age_")

df_female_wide <- profile_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Age, Scenario, Female_Prob) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Age, values_from = Female_Prob, names_prefix = "Age_")

# Generate the Male table
tab_male <- df_male_wide %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Scenario = "Risk Profile Scenario",
    Age_40 = "Age 40",
    Age_50 = "Age 50",
    Age_60 = "Age 60"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Table 3a: 10-Year Probability of Stroke (%) for Males by Age") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "Baseline Age at Prediction"), colwidths = c(1, 3)) %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  autofit()

# Generate the Female Table
tab_female <- df_female_wide %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Scenario = "Risk Profile Scenario",
    Age_40 = "Age 40",
    Age_50 = "Age 50",
    Age_60 = "Age 60"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Table 3b: 10-Year Probability of Stroke (%) for Females by Age") %>%
  add_header_row(values = c("", "Baseline Age at Prediction"), colwidths = c(1, 3)) %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  autofit()

tab_male
tab_female
```

# Longitudinal change of risk factor
```{r}
# Assume 'fram_long' contains data for PERIOD 1, 2, and 3
fram_long <- framingham %>% filter(PREVSTRK == 0)
# Continuous Variable Stability (e.g., Systolic BP)
long_summary <- fram_long %>%
  group_by(RANDID) %>%
  arrange(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_sbp = mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_sbp = sd(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_change = last(SYSBP) - first(SYSBP),
    # Within-Subject Coefficient of Variation
    wscv = (sd_sbp / mean_sbp) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  summarise(
    avg_wscv = mean(wscv, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_total_change = mean(total_change, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
long_summary
```

```{r}
# Prepare Wide Data
diab_cond <- fram_long %>%
  dplyr::select(RANDID, PERIOD, DIABETES) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = PERIOD, values_from = DIABETES, names_prefix = "P") %>%
  drop_na()

# Calculate Conditional Proportions: denominators separately for Baseline = 1 and Baseline = 0
summary_cond <- diab_cond %>%
  summarise(
    # Denominators
    Total_Baseline_1 = sum(P1 == 1),
    Total_Baseline_0 = sum(P1 == 0),
    
    # Numerators
    Persistent_111 = sum(P1 == 1 & P2 == 1 & P3 == 1),
    Persistent_000 = sum(P1 == 0 & P2 == 0 & P3 == 0),
    Incident_Early = sum(P1 == 0 & P2 == 1 & P3 == 1),
    Incident_Late  = sum(P1 == 0 & P2 == 0 & P3 == 1)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    `Steady Diabetes (1->1->1 | Baseline=1)` = (Persistent_111 / Total_Baseline_1) * 100,
    `Steady Healthy (0->0->0 | Baseline=0)`  = (Persistent_000 / Total_Baseline_0) * 100,
    `Early Incidence (0->1->1 | Baseline=0)` = (Incident_Early / Total_Baseline_0) * 100,
    `Late Incidence (0->0->1 | Baseline=0)`  = (Incident_Late / Total_Baseline_0) * 100
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = contains("|"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Percentage")

# Create the Flextable
ft_cond_diab <- summary_cond %>%
  dplyr::select(Metric, Percentage) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(Percentage, 2)) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(Metric = "Transition Metric (Conditional)", Percentage = "Percentage (%)") %>%
  set_caption("Table 4: Conditional Diabetes Transition and Incidence Rates") %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  autofit()

ft_cond_diab

```

```{r}
# Repeated measures ANOVA and effect size
library(rstatix)
anova_res <- fram_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(SYSBP)) %>%
  # Ensure only individuals with data in all 3 periods are included
  group_by(RANDID) %>%
  filter(n() == 3) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  anova_test(dv = SYSBP, wid = RANDID, within = PERIOD)

get_anova_table(anova_res)
```

```{r}
# Calculate Mean and SD for each period
sysbp_summary <- fram_long %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_SYSBP = mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_SYSBP = sd(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    N = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Mean_SYSBP, SD_SYSBP), round, 2))

# Create the Flextable
ft_sysbp_summary <- sysbp_summary %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(
    PERIOD = "Exam Period",
    Mean_SYSBP = "Mean SBP (mmHg)",
    SD_SYSBP = "Std. Deviation",
    N = "Sample Size (N)"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Mean and Standard Deviation of Systolic BP across Periods") %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  autofit()

ft_sysbp_summary
```

```{r}
sysbp_summary <- fram_long %>%
  group_by(PERIOD) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_SYSBP = mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_SYSBP = sd(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    label_text = paste0("Mean: ", round(Mean_SYSBP, 1), "\nSD: ", round(SD_SYSBP, 1))
  )

# Create the Plot
ggplot(fram_long, aes(x = factor(PERIOD), y = SYSBP, fill = factor(PERIOD))) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2, color = "gray30") +
  # Add Mean
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 18, size = 4, color = "darkblue") +
  # Add Labels
  geom_text(data = sysbp_summary, 
            aes(x = factor(PERIOD), y = Mean_SYSBP, label = label_text),
            vjust = -1.5,
            fontface = "bold",
            size = 3.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 1. Systolic BP Trends with Mean and SD Labels",
    #subtitle = "Comparing population shifts across three examination periods",
    x = "Examination Period",
    y = "Systolic BP (mmHg)"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1" = "#69b3a2", "2" = "orange", "3" = "firebrick")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  ylim(min(fram_long$SYSBP, na.rm=T), max(fram_long$SYSBP, na.rm=T) + 10)
```

```{r}
# Select a random subset of 100 subjects
set.seed(123)
sample_ids <- sample(unique(fram_long$RANDID), 100)

# Draw the trajectory plot
ggplot(filter(fram_long, RANDID %in% sample_ids), 
  aes(x = factor(PERIOD), y = SYSBP, group = RANDID)) +
  # Individual trajectory lines
  geom_line(alpha = 0.2, color = "gray40") + 
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, color = "gray40") +
  # Bold population mean line
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = mean, geom = "line", color = "firebrick", size = 1.3) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = mean, geom = "point", color = "firebrick", size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Individual Trajectories of Systolic Blood Pressure",
       subtitle = "Individual trends (n=100) with population mean overlay (red)",
       x = "Examination Period",
       y = "Systolic BP (mmHg)")
```

\pagebreak

## Code Appendix

```{r ref.label=knitr::all_labels(appendix == TRUE), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE, include=TRUE}
```
